name: Peggy Glass's Chocolate Lasagna
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine the flour, cocoa powder, and salt. Make a well in the center.
2. Add the eggs and vegetable oil to the well and mix with a fork until a dough forms.
3. Knead the dough for 15 minutes until smooth and shiny, adding more flour if needed to prevent sticking. Wrap well and rest for half an hour.
4. Roll out the pasta by hand or using a machine and cut into eight 4 1/2 x 11-inch strips.
5. Cook two strips at a time in boiling salted water for about 20 seconds after the water returns to a boil.
6. Plunge the noodles into cold water to stop cooking, then drain on towels in a single layer.
7. In a bowl, combine all filling ingredients and mix until smooth.
8. Assemble: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees with the rack in the upper third of the oven. Generously butter an 8 x 11 x 2-inch pan.
9. Alternate layers of noodles, cheese filling, and chocolate, ending with a layer of cheese. Bake for 20-25 minutes until the top is lightly colored.
10. Let the lasagna stand for 10 minutes to solidify before serving warm.
